seo.title: Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents in Claude Tool Use Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix tool loop detection in ai agents in Claude Tool Use agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Claude Tool Use agents, tool loops manifest as repetitive calls to the same or similar tools without progressing toward task completion, often triggered by the model's persistent tool_use content blocks in messages.create() responses. This occurs when Claude misinterprets partial tool results as incomplete, entering infinite cycles of tool invocation. The problem is exacerbated by Anthropic's stateless message API, where loop detection requires tracking tool call patterns across conversation history.

howItManifests.symptom: Claude calls 'query_database' tool 5+ times with identical parameters
howItManifests.impact: API rate limits exceeded, 30s+ response delays, incomplete task execution

howItManifests.symptom: Claude calls 'calculate' tool with results from previous 'calculate' calls
howItManifests.impact: Exponential computation growth, inconsistent numerical results, task abandonment

howItManifests.symptom: Same 'read_file' tool called repeatedly on identical file paths
howItManifests.impact: Excessive I/O operations, memory exhaustion, corrupted file states

howItManifests.symptom: External 'check_status' tool called without result-based termination logic
howItManifests.impact: External service rate limiting, increased cloud costs, stale data propagation

detection.name: Tool Call Frequency Tracker
detection.description: Tracks identical tool calls within conversation window using message content analysis
detection.code:
import anthropic
from collections import defaultdict

def detect_tool_loop(messages):
    tool_calls = defaultdict(int)
    for msg in messages[-10:]:  # Last 10 messages
        if msg.get('content') and msg['content'][0].get('type') == 'tool_use':
            tool_name = msg['content'][0]['name']
            tool_input = str(msg['content'][0].get('input', {}))
            key = f'{tool_name}:{tool_input}'
            tool_calls[key] += 1
    return any(count >= 4 for count in tool_calls.values())

# Usage
client = anthropic.Anthropic()
response = client.messages.create(
    model='claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620',
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Solve this math problem'}],
    tools=[{'name': 'calculate', 'input_schema': {'type': 'object'}}]
)
if detect_tool_loop(response.messages):
    print('TOOL LOOP DETECTED')

fixes.name: Tool Call Deduplication Middleware
fixes.description: Intercepts and blocks duplicate tool calls within conversation window

fixes.name: Max Tool Calls Per Turn
fixes.description: Enforces strict tool call limits per response with fallback reasoning
fixes.code:
def create_with_tool_limit(client, messages, max_tools=3):
    response = client.messages.create(
        model='claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620',
        messages=messages,
        tools=[...],
        system='Limit tool calls to 3 maximum per response. '
               'If more needed, explain reasoning first.'
    )
    
    tool_uses = [c for c in response.content if c.type == 'tool_use']
    if len(tool_uses) > max_tools:
        # Truncate and force text reasoning
        response.content = [{
            'type': 'text',
            'text': f'Analysis: Need {len(tool_uses)} tools. '
                   f'Prioritizing top {max_tools}. Continuing reasoning...'
        }]
    return response

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade tool loop detection by wrapping Anthropic's messages.create() API with vex.verify(), automatically analyzing tool use patterns across conversation history. It detects cycles in under 2ms using native tool call signature matching and enforces configurable cooldown periods without modifying your agent logic. Vex's loop detector integrates seamlessly with Claude's tool_use beta, preserving full message context while preventing rate limit exhaustion and infinite recursion.

The platform automatically injects loop-breaking system prompts when cycles are detected, maintains per-tool call budgets, and provides detailed loop forensics including call graphs and token consumption patterns. Vex's adaptive thresholds learn from your specific tool patterns, reducing false positives by 95% compared to static rules. Remediation happens inline - your agent continues with corrected tool selection instead of failing.

Integration requires one line of code and works across all Claude models supporting tools. Vex maintains full Anthropic API compatibility while adding enterprise-grade reliability guarantees.
vexAutomation.code:
import anthropic
import vex

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
vex_client = vex.wrap(client)

# Single line integration - detects ALL loop patterns
response = vex_client.messages.create(
    model='claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620',
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Process these files'}],
    tools=[
        {'name': 'read_file', 'input_schema': {'type': 'object', 'properties': {'path': {'type': 'string'}}}},
        {'name': 'query_database', 'input_schema': {'type': 'object'}}
    ],
    vex_verify={'tool_loop': {'max_calls': 3, 'cooldown': 30}}
)

# Vex automatically:
# ✅ Blocks loop #1: read_file('data.csv') called 4x
# ✅ Injects reasoning: 'File already processed, moving to analysis'
# ✅ Preserves conversation state
# ✅ Logs loop forensics to Vex dashboard

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your Claude client in one line to eliminate tool loops forever.
